title: Within 3 months all the zippers broke.
review_text: For the ridiculous amount of money Lululemon charges for their products they use by far the cheapest closures I've every seen. Through normal use after 3 months, 2 of the 3 sweatshirts she spent her birthday and Christmas gift money on no longer zip closed. The zipper pulls from the pockets broke off first, then one pocket's zipper's teeth misaligned so it will no longer unzip, and finally the main front zipper's cheap plastic stop completely came off so there is no way to zip it up. Shameful.

title: Item not received
review_text: Sadly I can't review the jacket, a Christmas gift for my granddaughter, because it was never received. It was free shipping and handling via fedex and tracking shows it going from location to location and even out for delivery on 2 different days at one point but never delivered. Fedex customer service assured me it was not lost and would be delivered but tracking continues to show it going between different local fedex facilities. Lulu Lemon customer care was great when I called them. I did originally send them an email about the issue, but never got a reply to that other than to give them 2-3 I think it was days to reply. They would have sent out a replacement item but unfortunately it is out of stock online and in all of the stores. I placed my order on December 5th and it is now December 20th. Therefore, without requesting it I was given a refund and an e-gift card as compensation which I received within 1-2 days. This was my first experience ordering online from them. I guess in the future, since I have 2 stores close by, that I will continue to buy from the store as I have always done previously. I feel sure that if the jacket had been received that my granddaughter would have loved it as much as she has all of her other prior LuLuLemon purchases.

title: The gift that wasn’t !!!
review_text: I wish I could provide a review of the product but unfortunately it was never delivered and multiple attempts to reach lululemon to resolve the situation have been unsuccessful. Purchased as a gift for my granddaughter, delivery was estimated for 12/22/24. Tracking information detailed the item “ loaded on a truck for local delivery” for 4 days, when on 12/27/24 the tracking information suddenly stated “ package damaged contact the shipper for further information “. I have tried repeatedly to call customer service, only to be put on endless holds. I understand they are not the one who damaged the package but they are the ones responsible for ensuring I receive the product or a refund what they charged me. It’s 8 days beyond the delivery date and still not one notice from Lululemon that my order was not fulfilled. However I have received multiple emails requesting my review on the product. How about you focus on ensuring your customers actually got what they paid for before spamming their inbox with review requests.
author: Beth G

title: Looks great but doesn’t last
review_text: The Nulu Define Jacket is very slimming and flattering. I bought the light pink (“strawberry milkshake”) one for my daughter’s birthday at the end of the summer and she loved it so much that she requested another one for Christmas (a grey-blue one with gold accents). So, why am I only giving three stars to an item that we liked so much that we ended up repurchasing? Because it doesn’t last. Despite us washing the jacket exactly as per the instructions on the tag, the fabric pilled after only a few washes. It also stained very easily. The pictures of the light pink jacket here enclosed are after five months of normal wear. I would not have repurchased it myself, but my daughter loves the look of this jacket so much that in the end the purchase was worth it.

title: BEWARE OF THE NEW "BLACK" COLOUR
review_text: I have several evolution polos and they are great; however, I just purchased the new "black" one and was HIGHLY disappointed. I returned it to my local store after one wear and one wash. First of all, the website says the polo is black, and even though the photo looks more like a charcoal grey, I ordered it. When it arrived it was indeed charcoal grey, NOT black. The second issue is that the polo is advertised as being "Silverescent® technology, powered by X-STATIC®" and "we added LYCRA® fibre for stretch and shape retention" ...which my other Evolution Polos definitely are; but alas, this new "black" one is NOT. Instead, it feels like it is 100% cotton, like any old run-of-the-mill t-shirt. And since it's all (or mostly) cotton, what do you think happened when I washed it. Yup! Nice false advertising, Lululemon. Even the guy behind the counter in my local store was rolling his eyes at this.

title: Evolution Polo

title: Disappointed!!
review_text: Firstly the shirt is delivered in a plastic bag which Lululemon should know is going to rip during transit so when I received it is already has accumulated dirt inside the the bag. Wore it for a workout then threw in wash and as others have said, it does stink and started to pile. Upset that I spent $100 on shirt that is definitely not worth the premium price point. Maybe these shirts are being manufactured differently and at cheaper rate but the quality has suffered against earlier products. Will stick to better performing Nike DriFits or UA.

title: Wears out fast
review_text: I love Lulu lemons gear but unfortunately I got these polos as a gift and they have worn out after only a couple of wears each. After just a couple of washes the material starts to bunch up which I would expect from a cheap polo but for this price I would want something that I can wear more than twice and have it look nice. The material it's self is really nice does great with moisture and fits great but I would not recommend this polo for this price given the defects

title: Frustrating experience
review_text: I purchased this shirt and loved it, except it was just a tad too long. I took into my local lulu store to have it hemmed. It came back and the stitching was coming unraveled at the hem. So I had to have it repaired. When it came back, it was coming unraveled IN A DIFFERENT SPOT! I hadn't even left the store since I wanted to inspect it to make sure it looked okay. This time it looked even worse. I am now waiting on the shirt for a 3rd time and hope that none of the stitching is coming undone, and I haven't even worn the shirt yet! I took a picture of the poor hem job this last time. Unreal.

title: Great Fit, Terrible Quality...Very Disappointed
review_text: To start, I"m a huge Lulu proponent and often recommend the brand to friends and colleagues. I own items ranging from the ABC pants to Surge shorts to Metal Vent tanks; and throughout my history of ownership, I have not had any serious issues with the quality of the items. In my search for a form fitting, multi functional polo, my first instinct was Lulu...where I found the Evolution polo. I tried on the large and instantly purchased two (gray and navy). At $88, my expectations were that it would fit perfectly and last for years...similar to other Lulu products I own. My first expectation was met. The fit of the Evolution polo is perfect. However, the Evolution polo missed my second expectation for quality by a mile. Purchased at the start of the summer in May, I wear each polo once a week (at most)...so less than 20 wears. I also wash and dry these polos with the same care as my other Lulu items. Unfortunately, similar to many other reviews, the Evolution polo did not hold up as there's significant "pilling" occurring in random patches. Ultimately, my initial love with the Evolution polo ended with a lot of disappointment. The $88 price tag warrants a higher quality, significantly longer lasting polo.
author: GSL1
lulu_response_text: Hey GSL1, We stand behind the quality of our gear and will be in touch shortly via email to follow up and help! Chat soon.
business_insight: Product material or durability concern affecting premium brand trust.

title: Fits well but early and excessive pilling is a major limitation
review_text: I own a number of different Lululemon products, including various shorts, pants, and short sleeve shirts purchased over time. Generally speaking, I've been quite satisfied with the look, fit, and durability of their products. That said, I own 3 iterations of the Evolution Polo which have unfortunately not lived up to the level of quality and durability I previously found with other products. Don't get me wrong, the athletic fit of the shirt is great and it was versatile enough that you could wear it casually, while golfing, or even at the office. The main issue with these polos is that all three of them have been prone to pilling early, even after just 5 wears or so. The most recent iteration of this shirt I purchased (shade stripe heathered dark royal heathered black) began pilling in multiple areas after only wearing it twice. I have stopped wearing my other two Evolution Polos due to the large degree of pilling all over each shirt. I follow the washing directions strictly and do not have this problem with my other Lululemon items. I have to say that while I was initially satisfied with these polos, their early and excessive pilling has left me pretty disappointed. Not what I have grown to expect from this brand in terms of quality and durability.

title: Get back to the way it was
review_text: I have maybe 20 of these. They all have a couple of distinctive features: a darker strip on the inside of the collar and down the middle around the buttons (usually black) and small buttons holding down the lapels. These features help make this polo unique. With this latest iteration of the design, Lululemon got rid of both of those details. Now these polos, which used to look distinctive, just look like a commodity. And frankly, if I wanted to wear a commodity, I would have paid $15 for these at H&M. Please make your design more distinctive, not less. I’ll be returning this latest version and will wait for you to redesign them. Thanks.

title: Much lower quality than before
review_text: I own 10+ Evolution Polos. Not only are they super comfortable, they hold shape we’ll so it’s neat enough to wear to work everyday. Not this most recent one, however. title: Quality downgrade from previous years
review_text: For the last several years I’ve purchased multiple evolution polos, I currently own over a dozen. This year’s design has absolutely taken a turn for the worst as profit margin has clearly taken priority over garment quality. The fabric feels cheap, the button under the collar has been removed (which makes it look floppy and unstructured) which is a real bummer as this was my favorite part about the shirt. The fabric trim lining the collar placket and neckband has also been removed... which also adds to the sloppy overall look. If the quality/design was the same as last year, I would have ordered every color you make (as I did last year). I’m now on the hunt for a new polo, from a different brand to fill my closet. :(

title: High quality?
review_text: I love lululemon and wear many of their articles of clothing. I love this polo and bought my first one late last season (don't wear short sleeves much in the winter). I love the feel, the look, the fit, etc. I've been looking to buy another—however, I am hesitating on this one because I'm starting to notice fraying in several places, including on the top of the collar where it's pretty visible. I don't think it's due to poor use because I always only wear this shirt on "to + from" occasions (no sports or gym use) and I always wash delicate and hang dry. Instead, I think it may be due to a fabric / material that is kind of fragile and susceptible to wearing more quickly over time. Like I said, I love lululemon but I would expect such an expensive and nice shirt to hold its own for a longer period of use.

title: Good, no longer great

I played in Mexico in extreme humidity and the shirt kept me cool while the collar buttons kept me looking fresh. No rolled up or flimsy collar like many polo shirts. I liked the color and pattern of the next edition Evolution polo so I ordered one and was a bit disappointed when it arrived. The buttons under the collar were gone, the black strip of material on the inside of the collar is no longer there, and the material is slightly different. I still like the shirt but removing the features that differentiated it from other performance material golf shirts makes it less of a standout.
author: MikeSD
lulu_response_text: Dear MikeSD, Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts on the Evolution Polo. We're glad to hear you’re loving the Evolution Polo. I understand you’d like to see the buttons and the stripe on the item still—I’d be happy to submit this feedback to our Design team for you.

review_text: I’m a bit concerned about this jacket from Lululemon. The overall quality feels great, but the “LULULEMON” lettering on the sleeve is already lifting and peeling, and I haven’t even washed it once. I take good care of it and wear it lightly, so I’m not sure why this is happening especially at this price point.
business_insight: Fit expectation mismatch that may increase return risk.

title: Fits short
review_text: Fits very short. Wish it was longer.
lulu_response_text: Thanks for sharing your feedback on the Pace Breaker Jacket. We hear you on the length feeling shorter than expected—that can definitely impact the overall fit. We appreciate you calling this out and will be sure to share it with our product team as we continue to refine fit and design.
business_insight: Fit expectation mismatch that may increase return risk.
